dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 7898 IH: National Guard Protective Zone Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mrs. Biggs of South Carolina (for herself, Mr. DesJarlais, Mr. Higgins of Louisiana, and Mr. Moore of Alabama) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on the Judiciary

form.official-title: To amend title 18, United States Code, to establish criminal penalties for interfering with National Guard protective zones.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the National Guard Protective Zone Act.

section.header: Interference with National Guard protective zone

subsection.text: Chapter 67 of title 18, United States Code, is amended by adding at the end the following:

section.header: Interference with National Guard protective zone

subsection.text: In this section, the term posted protective zone means an area around a member of the National Guard—

paragraph.text: the perimeter of which is not more than 15 feet from the member; and
that is marked by a verbal warning, visible signage, barricade tape, or other reasonable means.

subsection.text: It shall be unlawful, during a deployment authorized under chapter 15 of title 10 or under title 32, for any person to knowingly enter or remain within a posted protective zone with the intent to impede, intimidate, or interfere with the official duties of a member of the National Guard who is within the posted protective zone.

paragraph.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2), any person who violates subsection (b) shall be fined under this title, imprisoned not more than 1 year, or both.

If, in the course of committing a violation of subsection (b), a person makes physical contact with, throws an object at, or spits on the member of the National Guard, the maximum term of imprisonment under paragraph (1) shall be 5 years.

subsection.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed to prohibit activity protected by the First Amendment to the Constitution of the United States that is conducted outside a posted protective zone.
The table of sections for chapter 67 of title 18, United States Code, is amended by adding at the end the following:

toc.toc-entry: 1390. Interference with National Guard protective zone.
